member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw
member.summary: Defines static methods used to throw exceptions.
member.remarks: The main purpose is to reduce code size, improve performance, and standardize exception messages.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNull``1(``0,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified argument is .
member.typeparam: Argument type to be checked for .
member.param: Object to be checked for .
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNullOrMemberNull``2(``0,``1,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified argument is , or if the specified member is .
member.typeparam: Argument type to be checked for .
Member type to be checked for .
member.param: Argument to be checked for .
Object member to be checked for .
The name of the parameter being checked.
The name of the member.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfMemberNull``2(``0,``1,System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the specified member is .
member.typeparam: Member type to be checked for .
member.param: Argument to which member belongs.
Object member to be checked for .
The name of the parameter being checked.
The name of the member.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNullOrWhitespace(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws either an or an if the specified string is or whitespace respectively.
member.param: String to be checked for or whitespace.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNullOrEmpty(System.String,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the string is , or if it is empty.
member.param: String to be checked for or empty.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfBufferTooSmall(System.Int32,System.Int32,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the argument's buffer size is less than the required buffer size.
member.param: The actual buffer size.
The required buffer size.
The name of the parameter to be checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfOutOfRange``1(``0,System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the enum value is not valid.
member.param: The argument to evaluate.
The name of the parameter being checked.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.IfNullOrEmpty``1(System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{``0},System.String)
member.summary: Throws an if the collection is , or if it is empty.
member.param: The collection to evaluate.
The name of the parameter being checked.
member.typeparam: The type of objects in the collection.

member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.

member.summary: Throws an .

member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
A message that describes the error.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Shared.Diagnostics.Throw.ArgumentException(System.String,System.String,System.Exception)
member.summary: Throws an .
member.param: The name of the parameter that caused the exception.
A message that describes the error.
The exception that is the cause of the current exception.
member.remarks: If the is not a , the current exception is raised in a catch block that handles the inner exception.

The exception that is the cause of the current exception.

member.summary: Throws an if the specified number is less than min.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.DiagnosticIds.DiagnosticIds
member.summary: Various diagnostic IDs reported by this repo.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Shared.DiagnosticIds.DiagnosticIds.Experiments
member.summary: Experiments supported by this repo.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.OpenAI.StreamingUpdatePipelineResponse.Status
member.summary: Gets the HTTP status code. For streaming responses, this is typically 200.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.OpenAI.StreamingUpdatePipelineResponse.ReasonPhrase
member.summary: Gets the reason phrase. For streaming responses, this is typically "OK".

member.name: P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.OpenAI.StreamingUpdatePipelineResponse.ContentStream
member.summary: Streaming responses do not support direct content stream access.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.OpenAI.StreamingUpdatePipelineResponse.Content
member.summary: Streaming responses do not support direct content access.

member.name: P:Microsoft.Agents.AI.OpenAI.StreamingUpdatePipelineResponse.HeadersCore
member.summary: Streaming responses do not have headers.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.OpenAI.StreamingUpdatePipelineResponse.BufferContent(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Buffering content is not supported for streaming responses.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.OpenAI.StreamingUpdatePipelineResponse.BufferContentAsync(System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Buffering content asynchronously is not supported for streaming responses.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.OpenAI.StreamingUpdatePipelineResponse.Dispose
member.summary: Disposes resources. No resources to dispose for streaming response.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for and instances to create or extract native OpenAI response objects from the Microsoft Agent Framework responses.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseExtensions.AsOpenAIChatCompletion(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse)
member.summary: Creates or extracts a native OpenAI object from an .
member.param: The agent response.

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponseExtensions.AsOpenAIResponse(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentResponse)
member.summary: Creates or extracts a native OpenAI object from an .
member.param: The agent response.

member.name: T:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgentWithOpenAIExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for to simplify interaction with OpenAI chat messages and return native OpenAI responses.
member.remarks: These extensions bridge the gap between the Microsoft Extensions AI framework and the OpenAI SDK, allowing developers to work with native OpenAI types while leveraging the AI Agent framework. The methods handle the conversion between OpenAI chat message types and Microsoft Extensions AI types, and return OpenAI objects directly from the agent's .

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgentWithOpenAIExtensions.RunAsync(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{OpenAI.Chat.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Runs the AI agent with a collection of OpenAI chat messages and returns the response as a native OpenAI .
member.param: The AI agent to run.
The collection of OpenAI chat messages to send to the agent.
The conversation session to continue with this invocation. If not provided, creates a new session. The session will be mutated with the provided messages and agent response.
Optional parameters for agent invocation.
The to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is .
member.returns: A representing the asynchronous operation that returns a native OpenAI response.
member.exception: Thrown when the agent's response cannot be converted to a , typically when the underlying representation is not an OpenAI response.
Thrown when any message in has a type that is not supported by the message conversion method.
member.remarks: This method converts the OpenAI chat messages to the Microsoft Extensions AI format using the appropriate conversion method, runs the agent with the converted message collection, and then extracts the native OpenAI from the response using .

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgentWithOpenAIExtensions.RunStreamingAsync(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{OpenAI.Chat.ChatMessage},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Runs the AI agent with a single OpenAI chat message and returns the response as collection of native OpenAI .
member.param: The AI agent to run.
The collection of OpenAI chat messages to send to the agent.
The conversation session to continue with this invocation. If not provided, creates a new session. The session will be mutated with the provided message and agent response.
Optional parameters for agent invocation.
The to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is .
member.returns: A representing the asynchronous operation that returns a native OpenAI response.
member.exception: Thrown when the agent's response cannot be converted to a , typically when the underlying representation is not an OpenAI response.
Thrown when the type is not supported by the message conversion method.
member.remarks: This method converts the OpenAI chat messages to the Microsoft Extensions AI format using the appropriate conversion method, runs the agent, and then extracts the native OpenAI from the response using .

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgentWithOpenAIExtensions.RunAsync(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{OpenAI.Responses.ResponseItem},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Runs the AI agent with a collection of OpenAI response items and returns the response as a native OpenAI .
member.param: The AI agent to run.
The collection of OpenAI response items to send to the agent.
The conversation session to continue with this invocation. If not provided, creates a new session. The session will be mutated with the provided messages and agent response.
Optional parameters for agent invocation.
The to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is .
member.returns: A representing the asynchronous operation that returns a native OpenAI response.
member.exception: Thrown when the agent's response cannot be converted to an , typically when the underlying representation is not an OpenAI response.
Thrown when any message in has a type that is not supported by the message conversion method.
member.remarks: This method converts the OpenAI response items to the Microsoft Extensions AI format using the appropriate conversion method, runs the agent with the converted message collection, and then extracts the native OpenAI from the response using .

member.name: M:Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgentWithOpenAIExtensions.RunStreamingAsync(Microsoft.Agents.AI.AIAgent,System.Collections.Generic.IEnumerable{OpenAI.Responses.ResponseItem},Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentSession,Microsoft.Agents.AI.AgentRunOptions,System.Threading.CancellationToken)
member.summary: Runs the AI agent in streaming mode with a collection of OpenAI response items and returns the response as a collection of native OpenAI .
member.param: The AI agent to run.
The collection of OpenAI response items to send to the agent.
The conversation session to continue with this invocation. If not provided, creates a new session. The session will be mutated with the provided messages and agent response updates.
Optional parameters for agent invocation.
The to monitor for cancellation requests. The default is .
member.returns: An representing the asynchronous enumerable that yields native OpenAI instances as they are streamed.
member.exception: Thrown when the agent's response cannot be converted to instances, typically when the underlying representation is not an OpenAI response.
Thrown when any message in has a type that is not supported by the message conversion method.
member.remarks: This method converts the OpenAI response items to the Microsoft Extensions AI format using the appropriate conversion method, runs the agent in streaming mode, and then yields native OpenAI instances as they are produced. The method attempts to extract from the underlying response representation. If a raw update is not available, it is skipped because the OpenAI library does not currently expose model factory methods for creating such instances.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Chat.OpenAIChatClientExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for to simplify the creation of AI agents that work with OpenAI services.
member.remarks: These extensions bridge the gap between OpenAI SDK client objects and the Microsoft Agent Framework, allowing developers to easily create AI agents that leverage OpenAI's chat completion and response services. The methods handle the conversion from OpenAI clients to instances and then wrap them in objects that implement the interface.

member.summary: Creates an AI agent from an using the OpenAI Chat Completion API.
member.param: The OpenAI to use for the agent.
Optional system instructions that define the agent's behavior and personality.
Optional name for the agent for identification purposes.
Optional description of the agent's capabilities and purpose.
Optional collection of AI tools that the agent can use during conversations.
Provides a way to customize the creation of the underlying used by the agent.
Optional logger factory for enabling logging within the agent.
An optional to use for resolving services required by the instances being invoked.
member.returns: An instance backed by the OpenAI Chat Completion service.

member.name: M:OpenAI.Chat.OpenAIChatClientExtensions.AsAIAgent(OpenAI.Chat.ChatClient,Microsoft.Agents.AI.ChatClientAgentOptions,System.Func{Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient,Microsoft.Extensions.AI.IChatClient},Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.ILoggerFactory,System.IServiceProvider)
member.summary: Creates an AI agent from an using the OpenAI Chat Completion API.
member.param: The OpenAI to use for the agent.
Full set of options to configure the agent.
Provides a way to customize the creation of the underlying used by the agent.
Optional logger factory for enabling logging within the agent.
An optional to use for resolving services required by the instances being invoked.
member.returns: An instance backed by the OpenAI Chat Completion service.

member.name: T:OpenAI.Responses.OpenAIResponseClientExtensions
member.summary: Provides extension methods for to simplify the creation of AI agents that work with OpenAI services.
member.remarks: These extensions bridge the gap between OpenAI SDK client objects and the Microsoft Agent Framework, allowing developers to easily create AI agents that leverage OpenAI's chat completion and response services. The methods handle the conversion from OpenAI clients to instances and then wrap them in objects that implement the interface.

member.summary: Creates an AI agent from an using the OpenAI Response API.
member.param: The to use for the agent.
Optional default model ID to use for requests. Required when using a plain (not via Azure OpenAI).
Optional system instructions that define the agent's behavior and personality.
Optional name for the agent for identification purposes.
Optional description of the agent's capabilities and purpose.
Optional collection of AI tools that the agent can use during conversations.
Provides a way to customize the creation of the underlying used by the agent.
Optional logger factory for enabling logging within the agent.
An optional to use for resolving services required by the instances being invoked.
member.returns: An instance backed by the OpenAI Response service.

member.summary: Creates an AI agent from an using the OpenAI Response API.
member.param: The to use for the agent.
Full set of options to configure the agent.
Optional default model ID to use for requests. Required when using a plain (not via Azure OpenAI).
Provides a way to customize the creation of the underlying used by the agent.
Optional logger factory for enabling logging within the agent.
An optional to use for resolving services required by the instances being invoked.
member.returns: An instance backed by the OpenAI Response service.

member.name: M:OpenAI.Responses.OpenAIResponseClientExtensions.AsIChatClientWithStoredOutputDisabled(OpenAI.Responses.ResponsesClient,System.String,System.Boolean)
member.summary: Gets an for use with this that does not store responses for later retrieval.
member.remarks: This corresponds to setting the "store" property in the JSON representation to false.
member.param: The client.
Optional default model ID to use for requests.

Includes an encrypted version of reasoning tokens in reasoning item outputs. This enables reasoning items to be used in multi-turn conversations when using the Responses API statelessly (like when the store parameter is set to false, or when an organization is enrolled in the zero data retention program). Defaults to .
member.returns: An that can be used to converse via the that does not store responses for later retrieval.
